plan.title: Pre-created worktree resolver for swarm cohorts

narratives.Description: Adds reusable worktree-map resolution so the swarm can accept pre-created worktrees instead of always creating them inside the phased skill flow. It validates each story-to-worktree path against real git worktree state and the configured base branch, creates any missing worktrees idempotently, and emits a normalized mapping the launch engine consumes. Owning a standalone helper script keeps this independently buildable and unit-testable in isolation against the frozen C3 contract.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Add scripts/swarm_worktrees.py exposing a resolver function that validates a story-to-worktree mapping against git worktree state and the configured base branch, rejecting base mismatches.
2. Create any missing worktrees idempotently from the base branch and emit a normalized worktree-map JSON matching the frozen C3 schema that the launch engine imports.
3. Add tests in tests/cli/test_swarm_worktrees.py asserting validation, idempotent creation, and same-path collision rejection.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want the swarm to accept my pre-created worktrees, so that I do not have to let the skill recreate them on every run.

narrative.Acceptance: When given a story-to-worktree mapping, the resolver validates each path is a git worktree on the configured base branch and rejects mismatches with a nonzero exit.
When a mapped worktree does not yet exist, the resolver creates it idempotently from the base branch and records the created path in its output.
When two stories map to the same worktree path, the resolver fails with a nonzero exit and names the colliding stories.
When validation succeeds, the resolver emits a normalized worktree-map JSON that the launch engine consumes via the frozen C3 schema.

references.title: Issue #1387: feat(swarm): low-ceremony / headless launch for pre-approved cohorts (build on #1378 allocation-context token)
